steps.title: Identify the shared mechanism
steps.content: The researchers found identical bacterial genera in leaves and roots and offered two mechanisms showing how bacteria can move between plant locations: rain splashing soil onto leaves, and wind transporting bacteria.

steps.title: Determine the logical conclusion
steps.content:
Choice B directly follows from both mechanisms: if bacteria move between plant parts via the two pathways described, then identical bacteria in leaves and roots must originate from a common source.

Options A and D address topics not in the passage (benefit and vulnerability), while option C isolates wind deposits alone—narrower than what both mechanisms combined suggest.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
